children.heading: Subpena power
children.label: a Subpena power
children.text: For the purposes of this chapter, the Secretary shall at all reasonable times have access to, for the purpose of examination, and the right to copy any documentary evidence of any person with respect to whom such authority is exercised; and the Secretary shall have power to require by subpena the attendance and testimony of witnesses and the production of all such documentary evidence relating to any matter under investigation by the Secretary, and may administer oaths and affirmations, examine witnesses, and receive evidence.

children.heading: Disobedience of subpena
children.label: b Disobedience of subpena
children.text: Such attendance of witnesses, and the production of such documentary evidence, may be required from any place in the United States, at any designated place of hearing. In case of disobedience to a subpena the Secretary may invoke the aid of any court designated in subsection (h) of this section in requiring the attendance and testimony of witnesses and the production of documentary evidence.

children.heading: Court order requiring attendance and testimony of witnesses
children.label: c Court order requiring attendance and testimony of witnesses
children.text: Any such court within the jurisdiction of which such inquiry is carried on may, in case of contumacy or refusal to obey a subpena issued to any person, issue an order requiring such person to appear before the Secretary or to produce documentary evidence if so ordered, or to give evidence touching the matter in question; and any failure to obey such order of the court may be punished by such court as a contempt thereof.

children.heading: Fees and mileage costs of witnesses
children.label: d Fees and mileage costs of witnesses
children.text: Witnesses summoned before the Secretary shall be paid the same fees and mileage that are paid witnesses in the courts of the United States, and witnesses from whom depositions are taken and the persons taking the same shall severally be entitled to the same fees as are paid for like services in the courts of the United States.

children.heading: Violation of subpena as misdemeanor
children.label: e Violation of subpena as misdemeanor
children.text: Any person who shall neglect or refuse to attend and testify, or to answer any lawful inquiry, or to produce documentary evidence, if in the power of the person to do so, in obedience to the subpena or lawful requirement of the Secretary, shall be guilty of a misdemeanor, and upon conviction thereof be subject to imprisonment for not more than 1 year or a fine of not more than $10,000 or both the imprisonment and fine.

children.heading: Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 91–452, title II, § 20384 Stat. 928Oct. 15, 1970
children.label: f Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 91–452, title II, § 20384 Stat. 928Oct. 15, 1970

children.heading: District court jurisdiction
children.label: h District court jurisdiction
children.text: The United States district courts, the District Court of Guam, the District Court of the Virgin Islands, the highest court of American Samoa, and the United States courts of the other territories and possessions of the United States shall have jurisdiction in cases arising under this chapter.
